title: Which authentication approach should we use for the API?
context: The API will be consumed by both internal services and a web frontend.

options.title: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE
options.summary: Industry standard, supports delegated and app-only flows, integrates with Entra ID

options.title: API key per tenant
options.summary: Simple to implement, good for server-to-server, requires key rotation strategy

options.title: Azure AD managed identity
options.summary: Zero-secret approach, ideal for Azure-hosted services calling other Azure resources

project.name: Axiome API
project.description: Core REST API platform powering the Axiome product suite

title: How should we handle database migrations in the CI/CD pipeline?
context: We're using EF Core with a SQL database. The team deploys multiple times per day.

options.title: EF Core migrations at deploy time
options.summary: Runs automatically during deploy script, simple but blocks deployment

options.title: Separate migration job
options.summary: Dedicated pipeline step before app deploys, decouples schema from code

options.title: Blue-green with backward-compatible schemas
options.summary: Always keep old and new schemas compatible so rollback is safe

options.title: Manual approval gate
options.summary: DBA reviews and approves each migration before it runs in production

project.name: Axiome API
project.description: Core REST API platform powering the Axiome product suite

title: What level of test coverage are we targeting for this service?
context: This is a new service with no existing tests. The team has limited time.

options.title: Unit and integration tests
options.summary: Covers business logic and data access, good balance of speed and confidence

options.title: Unit tests only
options.summary: Fast feedback, low maintenance, but won't catch integration issues

options.title: Full pyramid including E2E
options.summary: Highest confidence but slower pipeline and more brittle, needs dedicated environment

options.title: Contract tests between services
options.summary: Validates API contracts between producer and consumer without full integration

project.name: Axiome API
project.description: Core REST API platform powering the Axiome product suite

title: How should we structure the Git branching model?
context: Small team of 3-4 developers, deploying to a single production environment.

options.title: GitHub Flow
options.summary: Feature branches off main, PR review, merge to main triggers deploy

options.title: Trunk-based development
options.summary: Short-lived branches, continuous integration to main, feature flags for incomplete work

options.title: GitFlow
options.summary: Develop, release, and hotfix branches with formal release process

project.name: Axiome API
project.description: Core REST API platform powering the Axiome product suite

title: Where should we store uploaded files?
context: Users upload documents up to 50MB. We need to support preview and download.

options.title: Azure Blob Storage
options.summary: Scalable, cheap, supports CDN and SAS tokens for direct access

options.title: Database as binary
options.summary: Simpler backup and transactional consistency, but increases DB size

options.title: Local filesystem with sync
options.summary: Fast local reads, but needs replication strategy for multi-instance deployments

project.name: Nova Dashboard
project.description: Internal analytics dashboard for real-time operational insights

title: What caching strategy should we use for the product catalogue?
context: Catalogue has ~10K items, updated a few times per day, read thousands of times per minute.

options.title: Redis distributed cache
options.summary: Shared across instances, supports invalidation patterns, adds an infrastructure component

options.title: In-memory with expiry
options.summary: Fastest reads, no infrastructure dependency, but not shared across instances

options.title: CDN at the edge
options.summary: Best for public read-heavy content, but harder to invalidate on changes

options.title: No caching initially
options.summary: Keep it simple until we have performance data, avoids premature optimisation

project.name: Nova Dashboard
project.description: Internal analytics dashboard for real-time operational insights

title: What observability stack should we adopt?
context: Running on Azure App Service and Functions. Budget is limited for the PoC phase.

options.title: Application Insights
options.summary: Native Azure integration, low setup cost, good for App Service and Functions

options.title: OpenTelemetry with Grafana
options.summary: Vendor-neutral, portable, full control over visualisation, more setup effort

options.title: Datadog
options.summary: All-in-one SaaS platform, rich APM features, per-host pricing

options.title: ELK stack
options.summary: Self-hosted, flexible, good for high-volume log analysis, operational overhead

options.title: App Insights plus custom dashboards
options.summary: Start with App Insights for basics, add Power BI or Grafana for business metrics

project.name: Nova Dashboard
project.description: Internal analytics dashboard for real-time operational insights

title: How should the service communicate with the downstream payment processor?
context: Payment operations can take several seconds. We need reliable delivery and audit trail.

options.title: Combination of sync and async
options.summary: Sync for reads and validation, async for mutations and reconciliation

options.title: Synchronous REST calls
options.summary: Simple request-response, easy to reason about, but couples availability

options.title: Async via message queue
options.summary: Resilient to downstream outages, supports retry and dead-letter, adds complexity

options.title: Webhook callbacks
options.summary: Processor calls us back when state changes, good for long-running operations

title: Should we build a monolith or start with microservices?
context: Greenfield project. Domain boundaries are not yet well understood.

options.title: Modular monolith
options.summary: Single deployable with clear module boundaries, easy to refactor, split later if needed

options.title: Microservices from day one
options.summary: Independent scaling and deployment per service, higher initial complexity and infra cost

options.title: Monolith first then extract
options.summary: Ship fast with a monolith, extract services along domain boundaries when pain points emerge

title: How should we handle configuration and secrets across environments?
context: We have dev, test, and production environments managed via Terraform.

options.title: App Configuration plus Key Vault
options.summary: App Configuration for feature flags and settings, Key Vault referenced for secrets

options.title: Azure App Configuration only
options.summary: Centralised config with feature flags and labels per environment, no secrets

options.title: Environment variables via Terraform
options.summary: Simple, infrastructure-as-code driven, but secrets end up in state files

options.title: Azure Key Vault only
options.summary: Secrets stored securely with access policies, but not ideal for non-secret config

title: What is the primary business case for introducing AI-powered automated cash allocation?
context: We currently match incoming payments to customer accounts manually. The finance team spends significant time on reconciliation, and mismatches cause delays in reporting and customer queries.

options.title: Reduce manual processing time
options.summary: Free up finance staff by automating the bulk of payment-to-invoice matching, cutting hours of daily manual work

options.title: Improve allocation accuracy
options.summary: AI matching reduces human error in payment allocation, leading to cleaner ledgers and fewer disputes

options.title: Accelerate cash application cycles
options.summary: Faster allocation means revenue is recognised sooner and cash flow visibility improves in real time

options.title: Scale without adding headcount
options.summary: Handle growing payment volumes as the business expands without proportionally increasing the finance team

options.title: Enhance customer experience
options.summary: Faster, more accurate allocation means fewer payment queries and quicker resolution when issues arise

project.name: Automated Cash Allocation
project.description: Automate reconciliation and allocation of incoming payments to customer accounts to reduce manual finance processing, improve accuracy, and accelerate cash application cycles
